gramGrp.iType: conclusive: -zu, -nu; adnominal: -nu; exclamatory: -ne; infinitive: -ni, -zu; gerund: -nito, -zute, -zuni, -zusite, -de (from EMJ); concessive: -nedo; provisional: -neba; conditional: -zupa; nominal: -naku

form.note: result of vowel assimilation, presumably: only as wago omop-, wago opokimi etc.
nwo reflects merger of no and nwo
yori is in EOJ and/or reflects merger of ywo and yo
pita is found once in EOJ

gramGrp.iType: adnominal: no; infinitive: ni, no; gerund: nite, nito, nisite; semblative: nasu, nosu

sense.def: [unknown; used in the makura kotoba 'swora mitu']

form.note: waro is an EOJ form, in 20.4343
kami reflects merger of mwi and mi and is also EOJ
ato likely reflects merger of two and to
sora probably reflects merger of swo and so
